name: ST MARGARETS HOSPITAL DURHAM BH2
units: metres, typed site-investigation log (Allied Exploration & Geotechnics Ltd, Form AEG21/2 cable percussion + AEG22 rotary), transcribed verbatim

intervals.verbatim: MADE GROUND (TARMAC).
MADE GROUND (Medium dense fill comprising of whole bricks, masonry with fine to coarse gravel and cobbles.)
Loose, brown, fine to coarse SAND.
annotations: log note: c.3.00m BGL... becoming darker brown and more clayey

intervals.verbatim: Soft to firm, dark brown, fine sandy clayey SILT, cross bedded with black fine to coarse sand sized coal fragments.
annotations: sampled U7 (undisturbed) 6.00-6.45m, N=11

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense, brown, fine, cross bedded SAND with occasional thin bands of dark grey silty clay and fine sand sized coal fragments.
annotations: log note: C. 9.50m BGL... becoming darker with depth with clayey silt bands.
log note: From 14.00m BGL... sand becoming coarse.
description continued across sheets 1-2-3 of 4 ('As sheet 1 of 4', 'As sheet 2 of 4')

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense to dense, grey brown, slightly fine sandy clayey SILT, cross bedded.
annotations: log note: between 18.50 and 18.75m BGL ... fine brown, very silty clay band.

intervals.verbatim: Firm, grey brown, slightly fine sandy very silty CLAY, cross bedded with some fine to coarse gravel and orange brown and green grey discolouration.
annotations: log note: from 25.50m BGL ... fine to medium gravel sized grey mudstone fragments present.
log note: becoming very stiff between 28.20 and 30.80m BGL.
chiselling 30.80-31.20m for 1.00h (base of cable percussion)
Cable percussion complete at 31.20m; rotary coring commenced at 31.20m
still classed drift: cross-bedded, gravelly reworked material above rockhead; grey mudstone gravel signals approach of the Coal Measures rockhead at 31.20m

intervals.verbatim: Grey, fine grained, fine sandy, highly to completely weathered MUDSTONE, moderately weak, with fine to coarse dark grey moderately weathered shale fragments.
annotations: rockhead / top of Coal Measures; rotary commenced at 31.20m (TCR 91, SCR 23, RQD 23)

intervals.verbatim: Black, lustrous COAL, fractured with very thin bands of mudstone, weak.
annotations: unnamed coal; 0.32m thick; no seam correlation given on log and no pencil marginalia

intervals.verbatim: Grey, fine grained, very thinly bedded, shaly MUDSTONE, moderately strong.
Dark grey, fine grained, very thinly bedded, moderately to highly weathered, muddy SHALE, moderately weak, highly fractured with occasional very thin black lustrous bands.
annotations: 'very thin black lustrous bands' = coaly laminae within the shale

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, fine grained, very thinly to thinly bedded, slightly to moderately weathered MUDSTONE, moderately weak, with occasional fossils.
Grey, thinly bedded, slightly to moderately weathered MUDSTONE, friable.
annotations: discontinuity: from 34.45m ... thin laminations of dark grey muddy sandstone.

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey brown, slightly to moderately weathered, fine grained, thickly bedded SANDSTONE, moderately strong, slightly micaceous.
annotations: discontinuity: 35.40-35.60m ... vertical orange discoloured fracture.

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey and grey, moderately weathered, very thinly laminated, sandy SILTSTONE, weak to moderately weak. Sand sized mica grain on laminae surfaces and occasional very thin interlaminations of black coal.
annotations: discontinuity: 36.25m ... some orange brown discolouration.
discontinuity: below 36.33m ... becoming slightly to (moderately) weathered and moderately weak to moderately strong.
discontinuity: 36.33-38.43m ... with closely spaced subhorizontal fracture occasionally infilled with a little clay.
discontinuity: below 37.13m ... becoming predominantly pale grey and slightly sandier.

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey, slightly weathered, occasionally very thinly laminated, slightly silty, micaceous SANDSTONE, moderately strong with very thin interlaminations to thin interbeds of dark grey, slightly to moderately weathered, very thinly laminated, slightly sandy SILTSTONE, moderately weak and with medium to coarse mica grain on laminae surfaces.
annotations: description continued from rotary sheet 1 onto sheet 2 of 3
discontinuity: below 38.43m ... becoming moderately weathered with a little orange brown discolouration.
discontinuity: 38.75-39.13m ... moderately weathered.
discontinuity: 39.45-39.53m ... subvertical joint, irregular with a little sandy clay infilling.
discontinuity: 40.13-40.20m ... highly weathered, friable and very weak.
discontinuity: 40.23-40.70m ... with closely spaced, subhorizontal joints with some sand infilling. Siltstone interlaminations becoming more widely spaced.
discontinuity: 40.70-41.30m ... moderately to highly occasionally completely weathered and often friable and very weak to weak.

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey, moderately weathered, occasionally very thinly laminated, medium to coarse grained, micaceous SANDSTONE, moderately weak to moderately strong, with coarse mica grain on laminae surfaces, with much orange brown discolouration.
annotations: discontinuity: 41.30-41.44m ... highly weathered with much brown and orange brown micaceous sand.
discontinuity: below 42.20m ... becoming fine to medium grained.
discontinuity: 42.30-42.81m ... subvertical fracture, with strong orange discolouration and a little sandy clay, regular, rough.

intervals.verbatim: Firm, dark grey, very thinly laminated, silty CLAY, discoloured green brown in places.
annotations: thin argillaceous parting logged as 'Firm silty CLAY' (0.14m) between two sandstones; classed mudstone as the argillaceous member
reduced-level marks: 27.55 at 42.81 (base of sandstone above), 27.41 at 42.95 (base of clay band)

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, moderately weathered, very thinly laminated, slightly sandy, micaceous SANDSTONE, moderately weak to moderately strong, with very thin interlaminations of pale grey, fine grained, slightly to moderately weathered SILTSTONE, moderately weak to moderately strong.
annotations: discontinuity: 42.50-42.81m ... with closely spaced, subhorizontal fractures, discoloured orange and with some sandy clay.
discontinuity: 43.45-45.81m ... with closely spaced, subhorizontal fractures, occasionally with a little sandy clay infilling.
discontinuity: 43.57-43.60m ... highly weathered, very weak and with orange brown discolouration.

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, slightly weathered, very thinly laminated, slightly sandy muddy SILTSTONE.
annotations: discontinuity: 43.74-43.79m ... 45 degree joint, regular, smooth, some broken, discoloured orange, siltstone infilling.
discontinuity: 44.36-44.40m ... 60 degree joint, smooth, regular, no infilling.
discontinuity: 44.68m ... subhorizontal joint, infilled with orange brown sand.
discontinuity: 44.90-44.98m ... 60 degree fracture, smooth, regular, with a little clay infilling.

intervals.verbatim: Black, moderately to highly weathered, lustrous COAL, very weak.
annotations: unnamed coal; 1.30m thick (the thickest coal in the hole); no seam correlation given on log and no pencil marginalia
discontinuity: 45.36-45.50m ... 60 degree fracture, smooth, regular, no infilling.
discontinuity: 45.50-45.63m ... subvertical fracture, irregular, smooth, no infilling.
discontinuity: 45.70-45.75m ... subvertical fracture, smooth, no infilling, in places a little orange brown discolouration.
methane reading at 47.51m = 1% LEL

intervals.verbatim: Grey, slightly weathered, occasionally very thinly laminated MUDSTONE, weak to moderately weak.
annotations: discontinuity: below 46.00m ... becoming moderately weathered, sandy, weak and with interlaminations of pale grey fine grained sandstone and some yellow and orange brown discolouration.
discontinuity: 47.51-49.07m ... with closely spaced, irregular, smooth, subhorizontal joints, often infilled with completely weathered mudstone.

discontinuity: between 48.32-48.38, 48.42-48.46, 48.78-48.80, 48.84-48.86 and 48.93-49.00m ... dark grey, occasionally very thinly laminated CLAY, with many gravel sized tabular lithorelics of mudstone.

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, moderately to highly weathered, very thinly laminated, in places coaly SHALE, with some very thin to thin interlaminations of black coal, very weak to weak.
annotations: coaly shale with black-coal interlaminations (not a workable coal bed)

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, very thinly laminated, silty shaly MUDSTONE, moderately weathered, weak to moderately weak.
annotations: discontinuity: 50.00m ... highly to completely weathered, friable and very weak.

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey, moderately weathered, occasionally very thinly laminated, fine grained SANDSTONE, weak to moderately weak, occasionally with very thin interlaminations of dark grey SILTSTONE.
annotations: discontinuity: 50.20-50.23m ... band of highly to completely weathered, friable very weak, silty mudstone.
Rotary complete at 50.57m (end of borehole)
methane reading at 50.57m = 4.3% LEL

notes: St Margaret's Hospital, Crossgate, Durham NE, BH-02 (also written 'BH2'). Client Ove Arup and Partners (Newcastle); contractor Allied Exploration & Geotechnics Ltd (AEG), Contract No. 1351; logged by G. Giles; status FINAL, dated 15.7.92. Boring 09-06-92 to 17-06-92. Ground Level 70.36 m AOD. Nine scanned pages: p01 = Site Location plan (Figure 1, 1:10560, dated 1967 base map) - no strata; p02-p05 = cable-percussion BOREHOLE RECORD sheets 1-4 of 4 (Form AEG21/2), 0-31.20m; p06-p08 = rotary DRILLHOLE LOG sheets 1-3 of 3 (Form AEG22), 31.20-50.57m; p09 = Site Plan (Figure 2, 1:500) showing BH01/BH02/BH03 and trial pits TP1-TP8 - no strata. The rotary sheets are stamped/handwritten 'Duplicate' (a copy of the drillhole log) but carry the full FINAL log. METHOD: Cable percussion using Pilcon Wayfarer 1500 to 31.20m, then rotary (air flush, 100% returns) to 50.57m. GENERAL REMARKS (verbatim): '1) Description derived from driller's report. 2) Methane readings at 47.51m = 1% and 50.57m = 4.3% no other methane present. (Methane Readings presented as Lower Explosive Limit).' WATER: cable percussion largely dry (water-depth column 'dry' 09-11.06.92 to 22m); standing water depths later 15.21m (at 25.50m casing, 11.06.92), 9.95m (12.06.92) and 18.84m (at 31.20m). During rotary, water standing recorded 24.48m (16.06.92 at 35.63m), 37.20m and 33.34m (at 42.81m), 45.30m (at 50.57m). Water was added to assist cable-percussion boring (2.00-14.00, 14.00-25.50, 27.00-31.20m). Chiselling 30.80-31.20m for 1h. GEOLOGY: superficial made ground (tarmac + brick/masonry fill) 0-1.20m over a thick glaciofluvial / buried-valley drift sequence of loose-to-medium-dense cross-bedded sands, clayey silts and a firm very silty gravelly CLAY to Coal Measures rockhead at 31.20m. Below rockhead, Westphalian Coal Measures: interbedded mudstone/shale, sandstone and siltstone with two coals - a thin 0.32m coal at 31.68-32.00m and a 1.30m coal at 46.21-47.51m - plus coaly shale/laminae at 32.50-33.00m and 49.07-49.67m. NEITHER COAL IS NAMED in the log and there are NO pencil seam correlations (LM/HM/BLM/Md/HTN/TGT) anywhere on the scans, so seam_marks is empty; the only handwriting is BGS registration stamps ('NZ 24 SE 1 316'), grid-reference jottings and the 'Duplicate' mark. ARITHMETIC: every interval cross-checks exactly - running thickness sum = stated depth, and every printed Reduced Level equals 70.36 - depth to 0.01m (e.g. 39.16 at 31.20, 29.06 at 41.30, 22.85 at 47.51, 19.79 at 50.57). No clerk slips. Final depth 50.57m matches the BGS index depth (50.57 m). The many 'discontinuity' fracture/weathering sub-notes from the rotary Detail column are captured as per-interval annotations by their stated depth range.
